Title: Search ongoing for man believed to have jumped off Banting bridge
Article:
KUALA LANGAT: Search and rescue operations are ongoing for a 29-year-old man believed to have jumped off a bridge in Banting, with no sign of the victim despite an extensive multi-agency effort.
Selangor Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Ashrul Riezal Asbar said the operation had entered its second day today, with firemen assisted by police, the Special Tactical Operations and Rescue Team (Pantas) and the department's K9 unit continuing to comb the area around the Banting bridge along Jalan Muhibbah 9, Taman Muhibbah.
He said monitoring patrols by boat covered about 5km from the search base, while the Water Rescue Team (PPDA) from the KLIA Fire and Rescue Station, together with personnel from the Banting station, conducted surface searches towards Teluk Datok.
"Pantas personnel and police also deployed boats to search within a 3km radius from the location, while the K9 unit remained at the scene to assist in the operation.
"Despite the extensive search effort, no trace of the victim has been found," he said in a statement today.
The incident was first reported at 10.33am yesterday, with fire crews arriving at the scene two minutes later.
Initial investigations found a blue Perodua Myvi believed to belong to the victim parked on the bridge.
Firefighters believe the man, aged 29, had jumped into the river.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran FM warns any challenge to Hormuz routes will 'increase tensions'
Article:
BAGHDAD: Iran's top diplomat warned Sunday that any attempt to bypass the Strait of Hormuz routes agreed with the United States would "increase tensions" in the Middle East, as the countries traded attacks and accusations of violating a fragile ceasefire in the region.
During a visit to Iraqi capital Baghdad, Abbas Araghchi also called for the establishment of a security framework with Gulf countries, with Teheran and Washington accusing each other of violating the fragile truce that was meant to end the Middle East war.
"Any attempt to adopt new or separate arrangements compared to what is underway by the Islamic Republic of Iran, will only lead to more complicated situations and delays in the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, and will increase the tensions, as we witnessed in the past two nights," Araghchi told a press conference.
Vessels have continued to use a non-Iranian-approved passage in the strategic waterway, tracking platforms showed Friday.
The Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) said a day earlier that Oman and the International Maritime Organisation (IMO) announced the new corridor without consulting Tehran, and warned vessels against using it.
Araghchi's warning came after the US military said it carried out new strikes Saturday on multiple targets in Iran, in response to a fresh attack on a ship transiting the Strait of Hormuz.
Teheran responded with launching strikes against US bases in the Gulf.
The recent clashes have tested the negotiating process meant to end a war launched by the United States and Israel against Iran on Feb 28.
Araghchi called on all parties to "adhere to the memorandum of understanding and not to allow this MoU to deviate from its course."
Iran's top diplomat said that "we should reach a new framework that includes all countries in the region and without the presence or interference of any country from outside the region."
He welcomed Iraq's call to hold a meeting between the Gulf States, Iran and Iraq, which was drawn into the Middle East war from the beginning.
Iraq is expected to hold on July 8 funeral processions for Iran's late supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed during attacks by the US and Israel on the first day of the war.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Age no barrier to service, say youngest and oldest candidates
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Being the youngest or oldest candidate in the 16th Johor election carries no special privilege, as both contenders believe age is no barrier to serving the community.
Pakatan Harapan's Danish Hossman Abd Rahman, 23, is the youngest candidate in the polls and will contest the Johor Lama state seat.
Perikatan Nasional's Lim Chin Eng @ Roland Lim, 73, who is contesting Stulang, is the oldest candidate.
Danish, campaigning as Danish Rahman, said that despite his youth, he had gained valuable knowledge from working with experienced leaders, which had matured him.
"I may be young, but I am no stranger to community work as I chair non-governmental organisations and youth groups.
"I learn from those with experience. As a youth, I am a bridge, taking experience from the older generation and turning it into aspiration for the young.
"I want to offer an energetic youth workforce and new ideas for the voters here in Johor Lama," he said today.
He said his decision to contest was also driven by a desire to change the perception among youth regarding the importance of politics.
Seeing many young people indifferent to national politics, Danish said he felt the need to step forward and raise awareness.
"I want to show young people that elections and politics must be taken seriously. Studying and working are important, but knowing about national politics is equally vital," he said.
Danish, who is from Pontian, is currently pursuing a Master's degree in Information Technology at Universiti Tun Hussein Onn Malaysia.
Asked about his preparations to win over older voters, Danish, who idolises Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, said he would focus on ground campaigns and face-to-face engagements.
"In Johor Lama, there are about 7,000 voters aged 60 and above, while those under 40 to 50 are more numerous. I believe the face-to-face method is better because it leaves an impression that I am a representative who will come and visit them," he said.
Danish also said he would focus on creating job opportunities for the people of Johor Lama so they would not have to leave the district or state to earn a living.
He also plans to assist local traders who frequently complain about poor business infrastructure and non-strategic locations.
"Johor Lama has a lot of potential, but it has been overlooked. Development has focused more on the southern part of Johor.
"I want a new state government that aligns with the federal government so that investment and development can be easily brought in," he said.
Meanwhile, Chin Eng said he believed age was not a measure of one's ability to understand youth, adding that experience was an asset in solving their problems.
"I am aware that the majority of voters in Stulang consist of young people. My approach is to go down to the ground, listen to their views, hold regular dialogue sessions, and ensure the voice of the youth is translated into action.
Meanwhile, Lim said he believed age was not a measure of one's ability to understand youth, adding that experience was an asset in solving their problems.
"I am aware that the majority of voters in Stulang consist of young people. My approach is to go down to the ground, listen to their views, hold regular dialogue sessions, and ensure the voice of the youth is translated into action.
"I do not assume to know everything. I want to be an elected representative who always listens and works alongside the younger generation.
"The combination of my experience with the ideas, energy, and creativity of the youth is the best formula to develop Stulang. I want to be a bridge connecting experience with the aspirations of the new generation," he said.
Lim, who is also Johor Baru Bersatu associate wing chief and Johor Bersatu associate wing assistant treasurer, said elections were not an age competition but a contest of the ability to serve.
He said he respected younger candidates who possessed the advantage of energy and new ideas, but he brought experience, networks, and the ability to solve the people's problems more effectively.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: What we know about the foreign victims of Venezuela's quakes
Article:
CARACAS: The latest death toll from twin earthquakes that rattled Venezuela on Wednesday stands at 1,430, and foreign nationals have been confirmed among the dead.
Here is what we know about the identities of the overseas victims:
28 Portuguese or descendants
Portugal's foreign ministry reported 28 victims of Portuguese nationality or descent in the earthquake and 85 missing, updating earlier figures.
Seven Chinese
Seven Chinese nationals were among the victims, Beijing's state broadcaster CCTV reported Saturday, citing figures from the embassy in Caracas.
The embassy posted a statement on its official WeChat account, urging Chinese citizens in Venezuela to take precautions against aftershocks and other "secondary disasters."
Nine Spaniards
At least nine Spanish nationals have died, 131 are missing and 14 others have been found under the rubble, the foreign ministry said on Sunday.
Spain had 147,000 nationals living in Venezuela as of January 1, according to official figures.
Two Brazilians
The foreign ministry of Brazil, which shares a border with Venezuela, said on Thursday that two of its citizens – a man and a woman – had died in the disaster.
The government was providing their relatives with consular assistance, the ministry said.
One Chilean
The foreign ministry of Chile, which hosts a large share of the Venezuelan diaspora, said Friday night that one Chilean national had died in the earthquake.
The ministry said it was providing their family with "assistance, guidance and support."
One Italian-Venezuelan
A man in his mid-50s, born in Caracas and holding both Italian and Venezuelan citizenship, was killed when a building collapsed in the hardest-hit region of La Guaira, Rome's foreign ministry said.
Italy estimates there are about 170,000 Italian passport-holders in Venezuela.
One Uruguayan
A Uruguayan citizen who had been living in Venezuela "for a long time" died in the disaster, the country's foreign affairs ministry reported Saturday.
The man's wife and eldest daughter were also killed, it added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Suspects in high-speed chase that caused crash in KL remanded
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The two men arrested after leading police on a high-speed chase before crashing into three vehicles along Jalan Kuching have been remanded for five days to facilitate investigations.
Kuala Lumpur police chief Datuk Fadil Marsus said the remand order runs from June 27 to July 1.
He said investigations revealed that police seized 19.63g of suspected heroin from the Perodua Viva involved in the incident, with initial findings indicating the drugs were intended for the suspects' personal use.
"The drugs seized are believed to be for personal consumption.
"The case is being investigated under Section 39B and Section 15(1)(a) of the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952, and investigations are ongoing," he said in a statement today.
The incident occurred at about 7.46pm on June 26 following a road crash involving four vehicles on
Jalan Kuching
, from the Duta-Ulu Kelang Expressway (DUKE) heading towards the city centre.
The latest development follows police reports that the Wangsa Maju narcotics investigation team had attempted to stop the Perodua Viva after spotting it in Wangsa Maju.
The driver allegedly ignored police orders to stop, triggering a chase that ended on Jalan Kuching when the car, driven recklessly through traffic, lost control and ploughed into three vehicles caught in a traffic congestion.
Both suspects, aged 40 and 42, were arrested at the scene after police recovered the suspected heroin from the vehicle.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Vance, Rubio strike different tone on Iran and Israel
Article:
PRESIDENT Donald Trump's administration has pushed hard to present a united front on the Iran war, but statements by his vice president and secretary of state have at times diverged over the past week, especially on the subject of Israel.
Vice President JD Vance, speaking at the White House last week, lashed out against Israeli critics of the preliminary US-Iran deal.
He suggested that Israeli bombings of civilian infrastructure in Beirut — intended to weaken Hizbollah, which has been attacking Israel — were undermining US-led peace efforts.
Secretary of State Marco Rubio, who travelled through the Gulf this week, defended Israel's military campaign in Lebanon, repeatedly describing its actions as a justified response to Hizbollah attacks.
Pressed on Vance's criticism, Rubio deflected before recounting an assault by the Lebanon-based militia on an Israeli checkpoint earlier in the week.
The contrast suggests that, even as the administration has emphasised unity, differing worldviews are at times rising to the surface — a challenge for a White House whose political coalition is deeply divided on foreign policy matters.
It also offers an early glimpse of the Republican Party's future, with Rubio and Vance both seen as potential 2028 presidential contenders.
Both Vance and Rubio were dispatched on high-profile trips abroad over the past week to defend the preliminary peace accord inked between Washington and Teheran on June 17.
Vance travelled to Switzerland for a round of talks with Iranian officials. Speaking to reporters on Sunday, he struck a decidedly optimistic tone on the state of talks with Iran.
He has also said repeatedly in recent weeks that Gulf states could fund Iran's reconstruction.
[[nid:1475104]]
He has also frequently mentioned the possibility of a new, more cooperative relationship between Iran and the US, revealing in an interview released on Thursday that the US had invited an Iranian intelligence official to serve as a deconfliction liaison with the Pentagon in Qatar.
Rubio, meanwhile, visited the United Arab Emirates, Kuwait and Bahrain to reassure allies — some of whom are concerned that the interim US-Iran accord is too generous to Teheran — that their interests will be protected.
On Tuesday, Rubio said he would not ask Gulf allies during his trip to fund Iran's reconstruction, saying such a possibility was "far down the road."
During a meeting with regional officials on Thursday, he emphasised that any deal has to be ironclad as it relates to US interests and those of its allies.
"While we want a deal, we don't want a deal at any price," he said.
The White House vigorously denied any divergence between the two officials.
"There is one camp – President Trump's camp – and the entire administration is fully behind the President's efforts to ensure Iran can never possess a nuclear weapon," said White House spokeswoman Anna Kelly.
State Department spokesman Tommy Pigott called the idea that there were any foreign policy divisions between Rubio and Vance a "tired and fake" narrative, saying, "The entire administration is 100 per cent in lockstep behind President Trump."
A separate State Department spokesperson further argued there was no divergence between the two officials on Lebanon, saying the administration's goal was to restore Lebanese government sovereignty over its entire territory.
Some analysts and commentators are unconvinced.
Michael Rubin, a senior fellow at the American Enterprise Institute think tank, said Rubio and Vance held clearly different views.
"At their core they represent different strains," he said.
The two officials come from radically different foreign policy backgrounds. Before taking office last year, Vance frequently criticised foreign wars as a waste of lives and money.
[[nid:1475103]]
Rubio made a name for himself as a "hawk" in the Senate, where he pushed for a more confrontational stance toward Iran, Russia and Cuba.
Both men are seen as potential successors to Trump and are the product of powerful, competing constituencies within the Republican Party.
On one side are "neoconservatives" whose adherents are more likely to advocate for foreign intervention.
On the other are Republican voters and policy professionals who argue that many recent foreign wars were costly and reckless.
Only 52 per cent of Republicans believe the current conflict has put the US in a stronger position, according to a Reuters/Ipsos poll that closed Monday, suggesting a party divided between those camps.
Both Rubio and Vance have nevertheless supported all of Trump's major foreign policy decisions, including his capture of Venezuelan leader Nicolas Maduro, his attack on Iran in February and his subsequent decision to pursue peace.
Both have even used similar talking points in recent weeks, saying they will judge Teheran's actions, not words, as negotiations unfold.
Asked by a reporter on Thursday to what degree his views on Iran differed from those of Vance, Rubio said they both took their lead from Trump.
"Everyone here is aligned behind the president," he said.
* The writer is from Reuters
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Driver charged over SPE flyover crash that killed motorcyclist
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A 21-year-old has been charged over a crash on the Setiawangsa-Pantai Expressway (SPE) that killed a motorcyclist who
plunged 30m from a flyover.
Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the driver, a woman, was charged at the magistrate's court on Friday.
"The driver was released on a RM1,500 bail with one surety. Her driving licence has also been suspended pending the disposal of the case.
"The court fixed Sept 10 for document submission," he said in a statement today.
It was previously reported that a 31-year-old motorcyclist died after plunging 30m from the flyover at Km15.8 of the SPE (northbound towards Sungai Besi) at 4.40pm.
The victim was identified as Muhammad Shafeeq Hashim.
Zamzuri said initial investigations found that the woman had lost control of her vehicle during heavy rain before crashing into the left shoulder of the road.
The vehicle struck two motorcycles that were sheltering from the rain under the flyover.
The impact caused the victim to be thrown over the edge of the flyover, and he was pronounced dead at the scene.
The
victim's wife
, Nurbaizura, shared her grief on social media, posting a final family photograph and expressing her heartbreak over the sudden loss of her husband.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thunderstorm, heavy rain forecast for KL, Putrajaya and 10 states
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are forecast to affect Kuala Lumpur, Putrajaya and 10 states until 7pm today.
In a statement today, the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) said the affected areas include Perlis, Penang, Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Padang Terap, Kuala Muda and Kulim), and Perak (Hulu Perak, Kuala Kangsar, Manjung, Kinta, Perak Tengah, Kampar and Bagan Datuk).
The adverse weather conditions are also expected in Gua Musang, Kelantan; Besut, Setiu, Kuala Nerus, Hulu Terengganu and Dungun in Terengganu; Cameron Highlands, Raub, Bentong and Rompin in Pahang; and Hulu Selangor, Gombak, Petaling, Kuala Langat, Hulu Langat and Sepang in Selangor.
The same warning has also been issued for Sarawak, covering Bau and Kuching in Kuching Division; Serian; Samarahan; Sri Aman; Betong; Sarikei; Selangau in Sibu Division; Mukah; Song and Kapit in Kapit Division; Tatau in Bintulu Division; and Subis and Miri in Miri Division.
In Sabah, the affected areas are Keningau and Tambunan in the Interior Division, Ranau in the West Coast Division, and Telupid, Kinabatangan, Beluran and Sandakan in the Sandakan Division.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pahang Sultan, Tengku Ampuan extend condolences to family of Tok Luar Datuk Jamaluddin
Article:
KUANTAN: The Sultan of Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah and Tengku Ampuan of Pahang Tunku Azizah Aminah Maimunah Iskandariah have conveyed their condolences to the family of Tok Luar Datuk Jamaluddin Ahmad, who died this morning.
According to a post on the Kesultanan Pahang Facebook page, Their Royal Highnesses expressed deep sadness over Jamaluddin's passing, describing him as a loyal supporter of the Pahang Sultanate institution.
"They also prayed that his soul be showered with mercy, forgiven for his sins, his good deeds accepted, and that he be placed among the faithful and righteous servants of Allah SWT," the post said.
Jamaluddin died at Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA) at 6.48am today.
The Sultan and Tengku Ampuan also expressed hope that his family would be granted strength, fortitude and patience in facing the loss.
"May Allah SWT shower His mercy upon his soul and place him among the righteous. Al-Fatihah," the statement added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: France records 1,000 excess deaths during record-breaking heatwave
Article:
PARIS: France has recorded 1,000 excess deaths during the blistering heatwave sweeping Europe, the public health agency said today, warning that the true figure was likely to be higher.
Detailing its preliminary count of excess deaths, Sante Publique said most of the fatalities involved older people and that it expected the mortality rate to rise as more information became available about deaths in residential care and homes.
Europeans have been enduring blistering conditions during a heatwave that has been linked to dozens of deaths - shattering records, disrupting power generation and damaging infrastructure.
Scientists have said the heatwave, which began on June 20, was ‌the worst recorded in Europe, where the climate is changing faster than the global average.
EXTREME HEAT EASES IN FRANCE
The heatwave has been moving east. But while France's weather agency said the extreme heat had diminished in most parts of the country, some areas in the northeast were still under a heatwave advisory.
Health Minister Stephanie Rist told La Tribune newspaper that the impact of the heatwave could linger for as long as 10 days after the weather had ebbed.
"The episode is not finished," she told broadcaster BFM.
Most of the deaths involved people aged 65 and older, though the health effects of the extreme heat affected all categories of the population, Sante Publique said. - REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls can trigger Umno-Pas unity through strategic voting, says Akmal
Article:
MELAKA: Umno Youth has welcomed Pas's suggestion for Perikatan Nasional (PN) supporters to vote for Barisan Nasional (BN) candidates in seats where the coalition is not contesting in the upcoming Johor election.
Umno Youth chief Datuk Dr Muhamad Akmal Saleh said the stand taken by Pas opens a new chapter towards the unification of the community, which Malay leaders have long envisioned for the sake of religion, race, and the country.
He said this development indicates that there is still room to strengthen cooperation between Malay and Islamic parties, especially in facing current political challenges.
"The Johor election can be a starting point for these efforts before being expanded to other states facing elections in the future.
"We see this as a positive development that should be welcomed with an open mind to ensure the agenda of empowering Malays and Islam can continue," he said after launching the Bromeliad Garden site here today.
The project is a collaboration between the Malaysian Pineapple Industry Board and the Melaka City Council at Taman Bunga Merdeka in Banda Hilir.
He was commenting on a statement by Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man, who urged PN supporters to
vote for BN candidates
in Johor election seats not contested by the coalition to block Pakatan Harapan (PH) from winning.
Tuan Ibrahim was reported to have said that the priority of the move was to ensure PH failed to win those seats.
"Yes, in places where PN is not contesting, we must stop PH from winning," Tuan Ibrahim said.
Akmal, who is also BN Youth chief, added that Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang had also expressed the party's intention not to assist in the campaign of Bersatu candidates.
Following this, he said Umno hopes the Pas machinery can campaign for BN candidates, particularly in areas contested by Bersatu.
"If they are not helping Bersatu campaign, I hope they help BN candidates. Therefore, I thank them and welcome this intention, and hope this recommendation becomes a starting point for a much better future for our country," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: BIS says debt, AI boom and fragilities raise global risks
Article:
LONDON: Global pressures ranging from rising public debt to financial fragilities and the sustainability of the artificial intelligence (AI) boom are increasing risks, underscoring the need for disciplined policymaking, according to the Bank for International Settlements (BIS).
The central bank umbrella group's Annual Economic Report, published on Sunday, warned of a complex mix of vulnerabilities, including strained fiscal positions, lingering supply shocks and the risk of a renewed bout of stubbornly high inflation.
While economic activity has remained resilient in recent months, policymakers must act decisively to preserve stability, the BIS said.
"Policy actions must reinforce each other to avoid a pull and push on the global economy. Ultimately, success depends on sound fiscal and financial foundations," BIS General Manager Pablo Hernandez de Cos said.
The report highlighted four key pressure points. Inflation has picked up again, with the BIS cautioning that more frequent supply disruptions could cause higher inflation expectations to become entrenched among households and businesses.
"The readiness to act if the central banks observe that there is the anchoring of inflation expectations is the main message that we want to set," de Cos told reporters.
He said the recent ceasefire between the US and Iran in the Middle East and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz were "good news" that meant extreme scenarios would be avoided, although it was likely to take time for the oil market to "normalise".
URGENT MESSAGE
The BIS also flagged uncertainty over the durability of the current surge in investment linked to AI.
While AI has boosted confidence and supported growth through expectations of productivity gains, the bank warned it was raising concerns about jobs and that supply bottlenecks and intense competition could lead to the kind of overinvestment seen in previous boom and bust cycles.
For central banks, it was posing fundamental questions about how the economy is likely to function, although de Cos said that, for now, it would be "unwise" to be prescriptive about how they should respond.
Financial vulnerabilities remain another area of concern.
Elevated asset valuations and signs of investor complacency have left core bond markets more fragile, while financing of the AI boom also appears increasingly reliant on debt and complex funding structures across the supply chain.
At the same time, record high public debt and sovereign debt markets increasingly dominated by large, highly leveraged hedge funds had created "a new sovereign financial stability nexus", which poses growing risks.
"The new fiscal financial stability nexus may mean more frequent and sharper drops in sovereign bond values," said Frank Smets, acting head of the BIS monetary and economic department, adding such swings could rapidly tighten financial conditions.
De Cos said the BIS' message was one of "urgency" regarding the need to bring down debt levels in key economies, "because the fact is that today debt is high, and this is financed through non-bank financial intermediaries."
The BIS urged policymakers to prioritise price stability, ensure fiscal sustainability, coordinate and strengthen oversight beyond the banking sector, and pursue structural reforms.
"Policymakers must act now. Delay will only make the necessary adjustments more costly," de Cos said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Loke: Underused LRT3 car parks to make way for affordable housing under TOD plans
Article:
SHAH ALAM: Vacant Prasarana-owned car parks along the Shah Alam LRT3 line will be redeveloped into high-density affordable housing and commercial lots under transit-oriented development (TOD) plans aimed at encouraging train commuting and optimising land use around rail stations.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the initiative aims to facilitate high-density living near stations, ensuring that more commuters settle within walking distance of their daily rail route.
He said several areas along the LRT3 corridor, including Sri Andalas, Kayu Ara, Bandar Bukit Tinggi and Johan Setia, have been identified for potential TOD projects involving housing and commercial centres.
"If housing is built there, we will integrate it with the station. Residents living in that area would not need another mode of transport; they could simply walk to the station," he said at the launch of the Shah Alam LRT3 line at the Johan Setia station depot today.
Loke said the approach serves three goals: making fuller use of government land, generating returns to help offset the cost of public infrastructure, and giving homebuyers the option of settling near a station to commute on foot.
He pointed to a typical 600-bay car park as an example of underutilised land, noting it benefits only a few hundred people while the ground beneath it sits idle.
"The redevelopment projects will retain parking facilities by incorporating them into basement levels, while the upper floors will house affordable homes and modest commercial units.
"If we just build a parking facility, the land is not optimised. Shifting parking into basement levels will free up the surface for housing and shops.
"These projects will involve the private sector and will not be developed by the government itself. However, the land belongs to the government through Prasarana, and we will work to keep the homes affordable, he said.
Four stations with the largest parking areas, Bandar Bukit Tinggi, Johan Setia, Sri Andalas, and Kayu Ara, have been identified for the pioneer phase.
Regarding the Sri Andalas site, Loke said the existing flat space accommodates 500 vehicles and is currently underutilised given the size of the land.
He said the plan focuses on fast-tracking suitable sites in areas with strong housing demand rather than redeveloping every station.
"If we can target at least these three areas, and have them expedited and implemented quickly, it will act as a catalyst for more projects like this," he said.
The announcement follows a directive from Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim for the transport and finance ministries to expedite approvals for
transit-oriented development
models.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Disabled man's creativity draws attention
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Losing his left leg due to diabetes in 2019 has not dampened Abd Karim Md Taha's spirit, who continues to brighten his home with creative decorations themed around current events.
The 59-year-old former lorry driver said his interest in home decoration began nearly 10 years ago, and ahead of National Day celebrations or other major events, he would transform the colour scheme and garage of his house according to selected themes.
He said among the themes he has created are Merdeka celebrations, elections, Malaysia Madani, and football seasons, including painting his house blue and red in support of Johor Darul Ta'zim (JDT).
"Originally, the house was soft orange and the garage door was plain.
"I would think of what could be done and start coming up with ideas based on current themes.
"For example, during elections I would put up flags and paint it in the colours of my preferred party.
"During JDT season, I paint it blue and red, and during Merdeka, I decorate it according to the national theme," he said when met at his home in Felda Sungai Sayong here today.
He said all decorations, including flags and paint, were purchased using his own money, costing about RM250 per project.
"Back when I was healthy, I did everything myself. Now I am no longer able to, so I provide the ideas and hire others to do it.
"Sometimes friends from the same block also come to help beautify the decorations," he said.
Abd Karim said he decorates his home not to seek attention, but as an expression of patriotism and as a hobby, as he is no longer able to contribute as he once did.
"When I hear there is going to be an election, I take out the flags I have kept since 2018 and put them up again.
"I have the spirit. If I cannot show support in other ways, this is what I am able to do," he said.
He added that his efforts had also caught the attention of Felda, and he once won a competition for the best-decorated house last year.
"During Merdeka season, my house often becomes a focal point for visitors who come to see the creative displays.
"I never expected this casual hobby to go viral, to the extent that Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate Datuk Seri Dr Adham Baba also expressed interest in visiting my home," he said.
Dr Adham is the BN candidate in the Johor election. He is up against Mohd Fakharuddin Moslim of Pakatan Harapan and Perikatan Nasional's Yuhanita Yunan
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: It's not just about MB candidate, says PKR youth leader
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The Johor election should not be reduced to a contest over who becomes the next menteri besar, but rather a test of which coalition has the strongest team and best plans to drive the state's economic and social development.
PKR Youth deputy chief Nabil Halimi said speculation over the next menteri besar was misplaced.
Earlier, Johor Umno Youth chief Noor Azleen Ambros said Johor deserved more than just a
menteri besar
candidate, but a credible governing team led by a competent and visionary leader, in an apparent swipe at Pakatan Harapan for declining to name its chief ministerial candidate.
"Perhaps Azleen has forgotten that Johor's 'poster boy' in the last state election also did not become menteri besar," Nabil said.
He said in the 2022 Johor election, Barisan Nasional (BN) campaigned under then menteri besar Datuk Hasni Mohammad, who ultimately did not retain the post despite leading the coalition to victory.
"People voted for BN under Hasni's leadership, whose performance formed part of BN's campaign. But the outcome was different, with Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi becoming the menteri besar," he said.
Nabil said he agreed with PKR vice-president Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa that the
Johor
election was about choosing the team capable of delivering a comprehensive economic and social agenda for the state.
Nabil said PH was fielding a combination of experienced and younger candidates, including former education minister Dr Maszlee Malik, former Johor executive councillor Mohd Khuzaan Abu Bakar, Pulai member of parliament Suhaizan Kayat, as well as a new generation of professionals such as Johor youth chief Muhammad Faezuddin Puad, Danish Hossman, Jalex Lee, Feliciah Poh and Ayna Soraya.
He said the line-up reflected PH's ambition to offer Johor "a fresher, more progressive and renewed state government" capable of strengthening the state's economy.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Hassan's appointment as Rembau Undang followed adat, no palace interference
Article:
REMBAU: The appointment of the 22nd Undang of Luak Rembau, Hassan Ab Hamid, was conducted independently without any royal interference from Istana Seri Menanti in Negri Sembilan.
Datuk Juan Datuk Zulkipli Shamsudin, chairman of the Kerapatan Buapak Lapan Kampung Waris Biduanda Dua Carak said that under Adat Perpatih (matrilineal custom), the selection of the Undang of Rembau remains the exclusive right of Luak Rembau, following established processes without royal interference in the selection of the ruling chief.
Zulkipli said the post fell vacant following the passing of the 21st Undang of Rembau, Datuk Lela Maharaja Datuk Muhamad Sharip Othman, who died of old age at 82 on May 15, 2024.
"Under the Perpatih system, the right to choose a new Undang lies with the descendants of Waris Biduanda, the sub-clan heads (Buapak), and the female clan elders (Ibu Soko) of Waris Biduanda Nan Dua Carak through a kerapatan (customary council).
"Following the council, the Buapak and Ibu Soko of Waris Biduanda Nan Dua Carak unanimously chose Hassan Ab Hamid, a respected retired teacher, as the 22nd Undang of Rembau.
"We then had an audience at the palace to seek consent for the proclamation ceremony, which was granted at Istana Besar Seri Menanti yesterday," he said in a statement today.
He said Adat Perpatih places customary power at the grassroots level, while the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan acts as a trustee to ensure the continuity of the state's customs and institutions.
He said the self-declaration by a man known as Abdul Rahim Yasin as the 22nd Undang of Luak Rembau was invalid as it did not comply with customary processes and procedures.
Zulkipli said he also failed to fulfil the requirement of completing his formalisation in accordance with customs by the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir.
Following the Istiadat Kejadian Undang Luak Rembau at Istana Seri Menanti yesterday, Hassan will defend and uphold Adat Perpatih, and ensure harmony between the institution of the Undang, the Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, and the people, said Zulkipli.
"We hope yesterday's ceremony opens a new chapter in elevating Adat Perpatih, thus putting an end to all unwarranted speculation," he said.
Yesterday, Tuanku Muhriz consented to an audience to proclaim the 22nd Undang of Luak Rembau at Istana Besar Seri Menanti, Kuala Pilah, where
Hassan
was proclaimed after his customary selection by the luak.
In his royal address, Tuanku Muhriz expressed his pleasure and consented to the proclamation of Hassan as Orang Kaya Beta, the 22nd Undang of Luak Rembau, carrying the title Datuk Sedia Raja.
"This appointment is a major responsibility born out of customary consensus. It must be shouldered with sincerity, wisdom and responsibility to preserve the sanctity of the custom and the well-being of the people in Luak Rembau.
"Any disputes or issues must be handled with wisdom, intellect and the spirit of muafakat (consensus)," the ruler decreed.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: As Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak rages, knowledge gaps still challenge response
Article:
AS scientists race to understand the growing Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak in Democratic Republic of Congo, medics say knowledge gaps are still hampering detection and care for sick patients.
Since the outbreak was declared in mid-May, Congo's government has reported more than 1,100 cases and almost 300 deaths, by far the biggest outbreak of the Bundibugyo strain of Ebola to date. There have also been 20 confirmed cases and two deaths in neighbouring Uganda.
"We're facing an outbreak… that we're just, to be honest, beginning to understand," World Health Organization emergencies director Chikwe Ihekweazu told a press conference on Wednesday.
There have only been two previous outbreaks, in Uganda in 2007 and Congo in 2012, which caused roughly 200 cases.
Ebola Bundibugyo is distinct from the better-known Ebola Zaire, which caused the largest-ever outbreak in West Africa in 2014 to 2016. But it is part of the same family of filoviruses and causes similar symptoms, including fever, vomiting and diarrhea, and sometimes bleeding.
The current outbreak was found late because the most common tests detect Zaire, not Bundibugyo. And unlike for Zaire, there are no vaccines or treatments to help tackle it, although treatment trials start next week.
The lack of data on how to spot the disease is still causing problems, Congolese officials and doctors said, particularly because the early symptoms are also common in other diseases like malaria, and seem to start mildly for Bundibugyo, meaning people may delay seeking care.
"For those at early stage of the disease, it looks milder than Zaire, and I believe that one of the reasons we are seeing more than 50 per cent patients at an advanced stage," said Abdou Sebushishe, a senior adviser at the International Medical Corps in eastern Congo, which runs several Ebola treatment centres.
Bleeding is also linked to later stages of Ebola, particularly in the minds of the community who experienced the Zaire outbreak in the same region of Congo in 2018 and 2019, said Dr Emmanuel Musingusi Bulemu, a Congolese health official investigating cases in the Nizi area of Ituri Province, the worst-hit region.
Around 40 per cent of Ebola patients experience bleeding, according to United States Centers for Disease Control and Prevention estimates.
But there are early signs bleeding is less common in this Bundibugyo outbreak, according to the WHO and evidence published by scientists at Congo's National Institute for Biomedical Research on Wednesday evening. They found only 10 per cent of people experienced the symptom, among 505 confirmed cases studied.
"As a result, people in the community say, 'Before, it was bleeding. We haven't seen any bleeding in our sick relative, so you're lying to us,'" said Musingusi.
"That perception strengthens resistance to the response efforts." Resistance and violence against Ebola responders have limited efforts to contain this outbreak.
Some scientists said that despite the differences between the Ebola outbreaks, similar measures were needed, including testing, isolating and treating patients.
"It's the same things," said Peter Piot, professor at the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine and a co-discoverer of Ebola Zaire.
"But I have never seen this many cases this quickly," he added, pointing out the challenges of responding in a conflict-hit region.
"Without peace I fear we will not stop this (outbreak)."
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: At 103, 'Death Railway' survivor still walks strong in Felda
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: At the age of 103, Kastawi Esa continues to walk on his own — carrying with him memories of surviving forced labour on the brutal 'Death Railway' under Japanese occupation.
Born in Kampung Punggur, Rengit, Batu Pahat on April 13, 1923, Kastawi endured a painful youth when he was forced by Japanese troops to leave his village and work as a labourer in Thailand during the early 1940s.
Kastawi recalled that at the age of 17, he and hundreds of other young men were detained by Japanese forces before being transported via Changi, Singapore, to Vietnam and later Bangkok, where they were forced to build the Death Railway linking Ban Pong and Thanbyuzayat in Burma (Myanmar).
He said life during the nearly three years there was extremely harsh, with forced labour carried out in dense jungle conditions and very limited food supplies, surviving mainly on river water.
"I did not know where we were going. We slept at night and worked during the day. We did not even know what day it was, including festive days such as Hari Raya.
"We worked under Japanese military control. If we made even a small mistake, we would be beaten with sticks," he said when met at his home in Felda Sungai Sayong here.
[[nid:1475077]]
More tragically, labourers who died from illness or injury were simply left behind without any treatment.
"Many died from skin diseases, boils and infections that spread because there was no medicine.
"I was fortunate, even though I suffered injuries while working in the jungle, they eventually healed on their own.
"I only used banana pith mixed with salt and applied it to the wound. That was all the medicine we had at the time," he said.
Out of eight workers from his group, Kastawi said only two survived and returned home after Japan surrendered to the British.
He later worked at the Sri Gading plantation before being invited by the government to join the opening of Felda Sungai Sayong on Aug 17, 1968.
"At that time, I already had a family with seven children. People told me not to go because plantation work had a fixed salary.
"But life in Felda was difficult. There was no electricity, roads were not paved, and the water supply was muddy and red."
However, Kastawi decided to relocate with his family as part of the early settler group, despite many returning to their villages due to malaria, extreme heat and poor facilities.
"Many people could not endure it and returned home. My family stayed because we believed life would improve."
That decision eventually paid off when the 4.05-hectare land he cultivated became a source of income that helped him raise nine children.
"After my wife passed away four years ago, I have been living alone, but my children take turns visiting me, bringing food and taking me to Friday prayers."
Despite being over a century old, Kastawi said he has no special secret to good health.
"I eat normally. I drink plain water, sometimes sweet drinks, but I do not drink carbonated drinks.
"All this is God's blessing. The important thing is not to neglect prayers, the Quran, and to perform night prayers according to one's ability."
Still able to walk unaided and free from any illness, he said life today is far better compared with the hardships he endured during the Japanese occupation and the early Felda years.
"We have gone through so much hardship. Alhamdulillah, life is much better now with more facilities, so we must be grateful and appreciate it."
The Death Railway was built by the Japanese during the Second World War to transport supplies and troops.
The 415km railway is widely regarded as one of the most brutal construction projects in history, claiming tens of thousands of forced labourers and prisoners of war due to starvation, disease and inhumane treatment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor campaign sees shift from street banners to digital outreach
Article:
JOHOR BARU: On the second day of the 14-day Johor election campaign period, physical campaign materials were noticeably subdued, with fewer flags, banners and posters along major routes compared with previous elections.
Political parties are instead shifting their momentum online, signalling a stronger focus on digital outreach and social media-driven engagement as the contest progresses.
The New Straits Times observed that traditional campaign visuals, once a defining feature of election seasons, are now less visible on the ground.
Johor Umno Youth chief Noor Azleen Ambros said the reduced physical presence reflected a change in campaign methods rather than a decline in intensity.
He said the familiar "flag war" culture was no longer expected to dominate the political landscape as in previous elections.
"Flag wars, banners and posters are not as prominent in past elections; now everyone is focused on digital media and online campaigns," he said.
The shift towards digital prioritises targeted messaging, particularly among younger voters, as well as rapid-response communication and content-driven campaigning over traditional street-level displays.
While physical branding remains in some areas, the campaign so far suggests a quieter but more digitally driven contest, fought as much on screens as on the streets.
As campaigning continues, attention is expected to turn to whether the subdued ground presence signals a longer-term shift in electioneering or simply a restrained opening phase before traditional political colour returns.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Baghdad Green Zone hit by massive raids as MPs, officials held
Article:
BAGHDAD: Iraqi security forces launched raids in Baghdad's fortified Green Zone early Sunday to arrest officials including MPs suspected of corruption, amid heavy deployment in the area.
The Green Zone houses the US embassy and other diplomatic missions, as well as international institutions and government offices. High-ranking officials and politicians also live there.
Video footage shared on local Telegram channels showed security forces in heavy vehicles such as tanks in the Green Zone, including a clip of them inside a compound and, in one instance, inside a house.
AFP correspondents reported heavy security deployment at the entrances of the Green Zone.
The large-scale raids, which included other neighbourhoods in Baghdad as well, were aimed at arresting senior Iraqi officials and politicians.
A security official told AFP on condition of anonymity that a "raid targeted several politicians over financial corruption, per judicial orders," adding that the operation involved anti-terrorism forces and the military.
Iraq's new agency (INA) quoted a senior official as saying that "several suspects accused of corruption have been arrested based on confessions made" by oil ministry official Adnan al-Jumaili, who was arrested last month.
The senior official, whose identity INA did not reveal, added that the arrests "involved members of parliament whose immunity was removed," as well as other officials.
Iraq's new Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi has vowed to fight corruption and mismanagement that have plagued Iraq for decades.
In a move to demonstrate commitment to fighting corruption, authorities seized more than US$85 million earlier this month in a graft case against Jumaili, with some of the cash hidden underground.
No official government statement on the raids has been released as yet, and INA did not provide further details or names of officials reportedly arrested.
- BEYOND CORRUPTION -
Another security official told AFP earlier the raids were carried out over corruption, as well as "funding factions and the smuggling of dollars and Iranian oil", referring to Teheran-backed armed groups.
Zaidi, who only recently took office with the blessing of the United States, is visiting Washington later this month.
A diplomat in Baghdad told AFP that the security operation "is part of the Washington visit preparations" to show Zaidi's commitment to his promises.
The raids also coincided with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi's visit to Baghdad.
Zaidi has also vowed to ensure a state monopoly on weapons amid growing US pressure to dismantle Teheran-backed groups, which Washington designates as terrorist organisations.
During the recent Middle East war, those groups intervened in support of Teheran and hit US facilities in Iraq, including an ambush on US diplomats in Baghdad. They also hit targets in Gulf countries.
He hopes to attract more US investment to Iraq, which urgently needs to revive its economy, especially after significant revenue losses caused by the halt in oil exports during the Middle East war.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Thai family mourns teen girl found dead in suitcase as Australian arrested
Article:
BANGKOK: The family of a 17-year-old Thai girl whose body was found in a suitcase in Pattaya said they were devastated by her death, for which an Australian man has been arrested and charged with murder.
Thai police said they arrested an Australian man in his 40s at Bangkok's Suvarnabhumi airport early on Saturday in connection with the killing in Pattaya, about 150 km (93 miles) east of Bangkok.
The suspect, identified as Simon Peter Carman, faces charges of murder, concealment of a body, moving or destroying a body, and taking a minor for sexual purposes.
Thai police said they reviewed CCTV footage that showed Carman entering a condominium with the girl, then leaving alone hours later carrying a suitcase.
In a post on its official Facebook page, police said he loaded the suitcase onto a motorcycle and rode to a grassy area near a railway line.
Police later issued an arrest warrant and arrested Carman at the airport as he prepared to board a flight back to Australia.
In an investigation room at the police station, Carman issued a message to the victim's family before being transferred to the Pattaya Provincial Court.
"I feel bad for what happened to your daughter. It was out of my control," he said.
The victim's father, Thongchai Donhomla, 46, said he was struggling to come to terms with the loss.
"I am deeply saddened. My daughter had no mother, so whenever she wanted anything, she would find a way herself, and she always helped me too," he said.
Her stepmother, Oradee Bussarakum, said she wanted the suspect to face the harshest punishment.
"I told the police I want him executed. As a (step)mother, I don't know what else to say ... I just want him to face the full consequences," she said.
Police have not said when Carman will first face the court.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Bersama ready to take on BN, PH in Johor Jaya
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Bersama Johor Jaya candidate Lau Yi Leong is unfazed by the risk of losing his deposit in the upcoming 16th Johor polls.
Lau, 33, said despite Bersama being a new party established only a year ago, he is determined to mount a strong challenge against established coalitions like Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN).
He said the progressive and independent stance of Bersama, which does not align with any major coalition, serves as a platform that can effectively represent Malaysia's multiracial society.
"I am aware of the risks faced by new parties, including the possibility of candidates losing their deposits.
"However, based on the positive feedback from Johor Jaya voters, I am confident we can challenge the other candidates, especially from PH and BN.
"This encouraging response has motivated us to campaign harder and reach out to more voters in the constituency," he said after visiting the Johor Jaya market here today as part of his on-the-ground campaign.
The Johor Jaya seat features a four-cornered fight involving PH, BN, Bersama and an independent candidate.
In the 2022 Johor state election, the seat was won by PH candidate Liow Cai Tung from DAP with a 1,922-vote majority.
The constituency has 97,685 registered voters.
Lau, a lawyer from Batu Pahat, pledged to champion local issues more vocally if elected on July 11.
Among the key areas he intends to focus on are worsening traffic congestion and the need for infrastructure upgrades in the area.
To engage with voters, Lau plans to utilise both social media platforms and physical campaigning.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Japan mulls expanding govt bonds for individuals
Article:
TOKYO: Japan's Finance Ministry is considering expanding its lineup of government bonds available to individual investors, with experts pushing for new products such as inflation-linked bonds amid rising prices, its officials say.
According to Kyodo News, bond purchases by individual investors have been soaring as the Bank of Japan (BOJ) moves to raise interest rates.
The ministry is also eager to diversify buyers of its debt given that the BOJ is reducing bond purchases as part of its monetary normalisation following a decade of unorthodox easing.
During a meeting among experts on managing government bonds, some attendees called for offering super long-term bonds with 20- or 30-year maturities to individual investors.
Currently, there are three types of government bonds for individual investors in Japan.
One is the 10-year floating rate bond, whose applicable interest rate changes every six months in line with market interest rates. The two others are five-year fixed-rate bonds and three-year fixed-rate bonds, whose interest rates remain unchanged until maturity. The interest for these bonds is paid every six months, and the principal is returned at maturity.
A ministry survey recently showed that the holdings of fixed five-year bonds have increased across all age groups. But the ministry wants to reach out more to relatively younger, working-age people under 30 as well as those in their 40s, according to a ministry official.
Regarding the proposed new products such as inflation-linked bonds, some at the ministry's panel comprising representatives of financial institutions said such bonds would be suitable in the current inflationary environment. The amount of principal increases for bonds tied to inflation.
Others pointed out, though, it is difficult to define the inflation rate, according to a ministry official.
In fiscal 2025 ended March, Japanese government bond purchases by individuals rose 36.9 per cent from the year earlier to 6.15 trillion yen (US$38 billion), the highest since 2006.
Sales of fixed five-year bonds were particularly strong at 3.20 trillion yen, jumping 131.8 per cent from the previous year, thanks to attractive interest rates, such as 1.66 per cent in the March offering, which is much higher than the interest rate of regular savings at banks, the official said.
The ministry is also set to expand government bonds to non-individuals, starting from those issued in January 2027, with an eye to targeting organisations such as private schools and condominium management associations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Zahid urges end to polemic over Puad's statement
Article:
BATU PAHAT: Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has urged all quarters to end the polemic surrounding a statement made by Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi.
He said the former Umno Supreme Council member's statement on the Barisan Nasional candidates contesting in the Johor polls is just his personal opinion.
At the same time, Zahid, who is also BN chairman, said he hoped the matter would not be raised further to avoid prolonging it and triggering retaliatory verbal attacks.
"It is his personal view and he is our friend.
"I think if there are differing opinions at this point, it is better to draw a line under it so that no further problems arise later," he said after launching the BN election machinery for the Parit Yaani and Parit Raja state constituencies at Dataran Tanjung Simpang here today.
He was commenting on reports that Puad had denied allegations that his decision to leave Umno was due to dissatisfaction over his son not being selected as a candidate in the Johor election.
Instead, the former Johor state assembly speaker said his decision was made in protest against the candidate selection system practised by BN.
Zahid also advised all parties, including Umno leaders, not to respond to provocation or engage in retaliatory attacks if the issue continued to be played up during the Johor state election.
"If an issue is raised, there will surely be a response.
"When this happens, a culture of retaliation is not good. I do not want issues that can cause embarrassment to be brought up.
"As Muslims, we should cover each other's shortcomings," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Iran, US continue escalating attacks, recriminations over peace deal
Article:
CAIRO: Iran and the US continued their attacks in the Gulf as each accused the other of violating an increasingly precarious interim deal signed less than two weeks ago to end their four-month-old war.
Shortly after President Donald Trump warned the US might "militarily complete the job", Iran early on Sunday launched missiles and drones on US military sites in Kuwait and Bahrain, continuing a series of escalating attacks.
Beyond the Gulf, Israel said it had struck Iran-backed Hizbollah fighters in southern Lebanon as fighting continued in an area Tehran says is key to its peace deal with Washington.
The US military said earlier it had struck Iran again, hours after a tanker was hit in the Strait of Hormuz, the world's most important energy shipping route, which Iran had largely cut off for most of the conflict.
The 14-point US-Iran interim agreement was meant to halt the fighting, which the US and Israel started on Feb 28, and reopen the strait to shipping while talks proceeded on more deep-seated issues, such as Iran's nuclear programme.
One round of mediated talks, led by Vice President JD Vance and Iran's parliamentary speaker, Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, was held in Switzerland a week ago and Washington then waived sanctions on Teheran, but the fighting and recriminations have since resumed and intensified.
"There may come a point when we are no longer able to be reasonable, and will be forced to militarily complete the job that we very successfully started," Trump posted on social media.
"If that happens, the Islamic Republic of Iran will no longer exist!"
About an hour after Trump's post, the Kuwaiti army said its air defences were responding to "hostile" missile and drone attacks, while sirens sounded in Bahrain, according to that country's interior ministry.
Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said its navy and air forces had launched missile and drone operations targeting US military sites in Kuwait and Bahrain in response to recent US strikes against Iran.
The Guards said in a statement the US strikes had violated the ceasefire and "will result in the complete halt of all diplomatic processes", according to state-run Press TV.
The IRGC navy command said American bases in the region "will experience hell in the coming days."
A US official, confirming the attacks on US facilities, told Reuters there were no reported US casualties or major damage to US sites in the Middle East but that the situation was still unfolding.
Hours later, alarms sounded for a second time in Bahrain, with the authorities saying an Iranian attack damaged a residential building in Muharraq province, with no casualties reported.
Bahrain urged the UN Security Council to hold an urgent session to hold Iran accountable. The Kuwaiti army said it had intercepted two ballistic missiles with no damage or casualties.
US Central Command said earlier that its forces had carried out fresh strikes after a Panama-flagged tanker was attacked by an Iranian drone on Saturday.
"Iran was given a chance to honour the ceasefire agreement but elected not to," Central Command said in a statement, adding that its strikes were "in direct response to continued Iranian aggression against commercial shipping" and targeted Iranian military surveillance, communications, air defence, drone storage and mine-laying facilities.
Iranian state broadcaster IRIB said explosions were heard in Sirik in southern Iran, without providing details.
The Guards said "America's blind shots at Sirik will not resolve our dominance over the Strait of Hormuz. But our shots at violators will remind the rest of the vessels of the clear passage route."
Saturday's tanker attack in the strait followed one on a cargo ship on Thursday that triggered the latest escalation.
Iran is seeking to assert control over the strait, which carried one-fifth of global oil and LNG supplies before the war and which had just begun to reopen after months of disruption.
Hundreds of ships, including tankers laden with oil, have been blockaded inside the Gulf since war broke out.
As they began leaving through the strait over the past two weeks, oil prices tumbled close to pre-war levels on the surge in supply.
Even as attacks continued on Sunday morning, CMA CGM's Galapagos container ship exited the strait in what the shipping giant called "an important milestone in a regional context that remains complex and requires constant vigilance."
Washington has been promoting a southern lane along the coast of Oman, while Teheran, which ultimately aims to charge fees for use of the strait, wants ships to use a northern route through its waters and under its control.
In Lebanon, Israel said on Sunday it had killed Hizbollah fighters armed with rocket-propelled grenades and struck a rocket launcher in the Nabatieh area. There was no immediate response from Hizbollah.
Iran accuses the US of violating its commitment under the peace deal to sustaining a ceasefire in Lebanon, which US ally Israel invaded in March in pursuit of Hizbollah.
Israel, which is not a party to the US-Iran deal, and Lebanon have repeatedly agreed to US-brokered ceasefires, the latest on Friday.
But these have had only limited effect, with Israel insisting it will not withdraw from Lebanese territory it has seized and Hizbollah repeatedly rejecting calls to give up its arms as long as Israeli troops remain in place.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election: Zahid tells Pas to turn words into votes for BN
Article:
BATU PAHAT: Pas should translate its words into action if the Islamist party is sincere about throwing its support behind Barisan Nasional (BN) candidates contesting in the Johor election, Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said.
The BN chairman, who is also UMNO president, said if Pas was sincere, it should translate those words into votes for BN candidates contesting in the election.
Zahid nevertheless welcomed the call made by a top Pas leader for Perikatan Nasional (PN) members and supporters to vote for BN candidates in seats not contested by the party in the Johor election.
"It is good if they support all 56 BN candidates contesting in Johor.
"I hope those words will be fulfilled and translated into action.
"We do not want it to be merely paying lip service," he told reporters after launching the BN election machinery for the Parit Yaani and Parit Raja constituencies at Dataran Tanjung Simpang here today.
Earlier today in Kota Baru, Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man called on PN supporters to vote for Umno at seats where the coalition is not fielding candidates.
Tuan Ibrahim said the directive was aimed at preventing vote-splitting and strengthening Pakatan Harapan (PH).
"In constituencies where PN is not fielding candidates, we ask our supporters to vote for Umno," he said.
PN is contesting 33 seats in the July 11 election, with half of the seats contested by Bersatu. Pas is fielding candidates in 11 seats, followed by the Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP) with five.
Parti Pejuang Tanah Air, which was recently accepted into PN, is contesting the Gambir seat.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Make me look good': Ahmad Maslan asks reporter for a touch-up before Johor election presser
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A spontaneous moment of humour between a veteran politician and members of the media brought a touch of levity to the hectic Johor election campaign trail.
Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan lightened the mood ahead of a press conference when he jokingly asked journalists if his face was sweaty and needed a touch-up before cameras started rolling.
Kosmo! journalist Mohd Sharkawi Londing said Ahmad had asked for someone to help to wipe away his sweat, after which the interaction turned into a light-hearted exchange.
"He first asked if there was sweat on his face because he didn't want to appear like that on camera. I helped wipe it off and then fixed his hair a little," he said.
"What started with wiping sweat ended with me combing his hair. It became a fun moment amid a very busy election period," he added.
Ahmad was in Pontian earlier to officiate the "Meh Lah Pontian: Kelana Keringat" community programme held in conjunction with the International Museum Day Festival at Tampok Laut Recreational Centre.
Sharkawi, who has over a decade of experience in journalism, said the light-hearted exchange was a welcome breather amid the demanding election coverage schedule.
"During this busy election season, moments like this help ease the pressure of work and bring some cheer," he said.
The Johor polls election campaign continues under a two-week period following nomination day, with early voting set for July 7 and polling day on July 11.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election victory crucial for Umno revival, says Zahid
Article:
BATU PAHAT: Umno must secure victory in the Johor polls as the starting point for the party's revival after a difficult period following the 14th General Election (GE14) eight years ago.
Its president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, who is also Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman, said victory for Umno and BN was also crucial to ensure Johor remains the party's 'fixed-deposit' (stronghold).
"What is important is that we must first ensure our candidates win, Insya-Allah, without arrogance or pride.
"We remind ourselves that Umno and BN will make a real comeback starting from this blessed land of Johor.
"But when there are state elections in Johor and later in Negri Sembilan, do not put pressure on us… do not block us from entering Felda areas.
"Let us meet on the political field in a gentlemanly manner. What is this, blocking Umno on Facebook and TikTok? Let us compete until we win," he said when launching the BN machinery for the Parit Yaani and Parit Raja state constituencies here today.
Also present were BN secretary-general Datuk Dr Zambry Abd Kadir, BN candidate for Parit Yaani Datuk Najib Samuri, and BN candidate for Parit Raja Rashidah Ramli.
Zahid said the political reality in Johor was one of stability, clear strategy and firm focus, which had led both foreign and domestic investors to regard the state as an important investment destination.
At the same time, he aslo recognised the contributions of former menteris besar and previous administrations, who had played a role in the state's development.
"We do not deny their contributions. But since BN returned to power in Johor under Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, I see it as extraordinary.
"This is the reality, and there are not many issues raised in Johor. Not many problems… it is not difficult to win.
"On May 11, we celebrated Umno's 80th anniversary. Umno was founded at the Johor Bahru palace. This means Umno is not a party of the streets… not a family-based party.
"Umno is a party of all Malaysians," he said.
Asked on BN's campaign on the second day of the election, Zahid said everything was running smoothly and there had been no personal attacks or sensitive issues raised so far.
"If everyone continues with this approach, God willing there will be no problems for voters.
"Besides working politically, we also pray to Allah that what we are doing will bring extraordinary success," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Budi Madani portal receives 13,000 new applications as off 11am - MOF
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The official Budi Madani portal has received over 13,000 new applications as of 11am today for an additional 100 litres of subsidised diesel per month for eligible private pick-up truck and jeep owners.
The Finance Ministry (MOF), in a statement today, said this addition increases the total diesel subsidy quota to 300 litres per month for eligible vehicle owners.
"The first day of the early access rollout for the Budi Madani Diesel (Budi Diesel) initiative for eligible private diesel vehicle owners in Peninsular Malaysia on June 27 ran smoothly.
The MyKad verification system at petrol stations worked well, with no serious complaints or issues reported.
"Throughout the first day of early access on June 27, nearly 24,000 transactions were recorded, involving the purchase of almost one million litres of subsidised diesel worth RM2.1 million at RM2.15 per litre," the statement read.
MOF added that the early access rollout prepares for the full nationwide implementation of Budi Diesel starting July 1, which will enable eligible citizens to purchase subsidised diesel at RM2.10 per litre via MyKad verification.
The ministry said diesel subsidy rationalisation is implemented to ensure the country's subsidised diesel supply remains sufficient for citizens and sectors that genuinely need it. It also aims to curb leakages and ensure targeted delivery.
"For the public transport, logistics and eligible commercial vehicle sectors, the diesel subsidy will continue to be channelled through the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) under the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry, according to their respective categories and operational needs," the MOF statement added.
It said the government will continue to monitor the Budi Diesel early access phase and fine-tune its execution based on feedback from the public and industry.
"The public can check their eligibility status, apply for the additional Budi Diesel quota or obtain further information through the official Budi Madani portal, while SKDS applications can be made through KPDN's MySubsidi portal," said the MOF.
– Bernama
TAGS: BUDI MADANI, application, quota, diesel, jeep, pick-up
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Delivery dispute leads to assault on rider in Petaling Jaya, police say
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Police believe a misunderstanding over a food delivery, coupled with alcohol consumption, led to the assault of a food delivery rider in Kelana Jaya early on Friday.
Petaling Jaya deputy police chief Superintendent M. Hussin Sollehuddin Zolkifly said investigations found the incident was believed to have stemmed from a dispute between the victim and the suspects over the delivery.
"The suspect was also believed to have been intoxicated at the time of the incident.
"We are also tracking down another suspect believed to have been involved in the assault," he said in a statement today.
Yesterday,
police confirmed the arrest of a man in his 30s
to assist investigations into the assault.
The victim, who wished to be known only as Man, said he had accepted an order from a fast-food restaurant at a petrol station in SS6, Kelana Jaya, at 4.45am for delivery to premises in Taman Rasa Sayang.
He said that upon arriving at 5.30am, he was unable to locate the premises and contacted the customer for directions.
"I called the customer, but he did not answer my questions. He only kept asking whether I had arrived downstairs before hanging up.
"I tried calling again and he became angry, asking why I was waiting at the end of the row of shops. The area was dark and I genuinely could not see where they were," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pakatan’s youngest candidate Danish Hossman takes cue from PM Anwar in Johor Lama showdown
Article:
KOTA TINGGI, June 28 — The resilience and staunch principles of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s struggle served as the main ‘tonic’ to inspire Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Danish Hossman Abd Rahman to enter politics at the young age of 23.
Contesting the Johor Lama State Legislative Assembly (DUN) seat in the 16th Johor State Election (PRN), Danish Hossman, who is the youngest candidate, described the PH Chairman as his political ‘compass’ — one who has remained steadfast in championing the welfare of the people despite facing various storms of trials.
“To be honest, I only have one idol in this political arena, Datuk Seri Anwar. We look at his political career from the very beginning; it has always been about fighting for the rights of the people, even to the point of being dismissed from office and imprisoned.
“I came in during a time when the economy is more stable and the ringgit is performing better. I did not experience the hardships that he went through, but I deeply respect how he endured for decades for the sake of fighting for the people,” he said in an exclusive interview with Bernama here today.
The Master of Information Technology student from Universiti Tun Hussein Onn Malaysia (UTHM) said his interest in the political world did not emerge overnight; rather, it is rooted in his family background, which has been active in the field since his school days.
“I was born into a political family. My grandfather was an Umno chief, while my father was the Pontian PKR Branch Chief for 10 years, and I was raised in an environment constantly exposed to politics, economics, and the issues of the people. That is where my interest in entering politics stemmed from,” the second-year UTHM student said.
Danish Hossman, who is also the Pontian PKR Branch Secretary and a Johor Angkatan Muda Keadilan (AMK) Exco Member, stated that he did not join politics to chase fame or positions, but rather to serve the people.
The eldest of five siblings is no stranger to community work, having long honed his leadership skills through active involvement in various non-governmental organisations (NGOs) at the state level.
Commenting on the scepticism from certain quarters regarding his age factor, Danish Hossman considers it an asset rather than an obstacle, as it allows him to bring a new, more inclusive dimension and perspective.
“We can offer fresh ideas, and we can draw on long-standing experiences, but whatever the case, our main goal is to be a state assemblyman who works and listens to the people’s issues. That is what matters,” he said.
Danish Hossman is set to face a three-cornered contest against the incumbent for the Johor Lama seat, Norlizah Noh from Barisan Nasional (BN), as well as Perikatan Nasional (PN) candidate Aisah Esa on July 11.
Despite being aware that he will be going up against big names in the Johor Lama constituency, Danish Hossman remains unfazed and is optimistic that the mindset landscape of voters has shifted and grown more mature in evaluating information.
He noted that technological advancements and rapid access to information allow the residents of the Johor Lama constituency to objectively judge the performance and offerings of each party for themselves.
“It is the internet era now; they can look up information on their own and see developments on the outside for themselves. They can judge what is good and what is not.
“Insya-Allah (God willing), Pakatan Harapan still has a chance here, and I will meet as many voters as possible to hear their problems firsthand,” he said.
The Johor State Election will take place on July 11, with early voting scheduled for July 7.
To get the latest updates on the 16th Johor State Election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor. — Bernama

Title: Amir Hamzah unveils RM5.5m sea ambulance, promises faster patient transfers from Langkawi
Article:
LANGKAWI, June 28 — The Ministry of Finance (MOF) has allocated RM5.5 million for the procurement of a sea ambulance for Langkawi and its initial operating costs, with the service expected to commence early next year.
Finance Minister II Datuk Seri Amir Hamzah Azizan said the initiative aimed to strengthen access to faster and more efficient emergency healthcare services, particularly for the Langkawi community and tourists.
Amir Hamzah, who is also co-chairman of the Langkawi Development Authority (LADA), said the sea ambulance would be operated by Hospital Sultanah Maliha (HSM) here, with its primary role being the transfer of patients from the hospital to the mainland.
“At present, patient transfers from Langkawi to referral hospitals on the mainland still depend on passenger ferry services.
“This becomes even more challenging when ferry frequencies are reduced, limiting travel options, especially in emergency situations requiring immediate patient transfers,” he told reporters here today.
Earlier, he attended the presentation of RM700,000 worth of healthcare equipment contributed by MOF to HSM.
He said the sea ambulance service would also ensure the health, safety and privacy of patients during transfers.
“I understand that the sea ambulance can accommodate two stretchers and 12 personnel who will ensure patients remain comfortable and safe during the transfer,” he said.
On the healthcare equipment contributed to HSM, Amir Hamzah said it was aimed at strengthening the hospital’s capacity to provide more efficient, modern and high-quality healthcare services to Langkawi residents and the millions of tourists visiting the island each year.
He said the equipment includes medical devices, information technology (IT) facilities, furniture, vehicles and security systems.
“We welcome the government’s new initiatives, including digitalisation, where the Electronic Medical Record (EMR) system will become a key pillar of the Health Ministry.
“We are providing computers on wheels, enabling nurses and doctors to enter patients’ data directly into the system during ward rounds, while connecting this hospital with other hospitals.
